title: Input and accessibility baseline
summary: Validate baseline keyboard/mouse input, UI scaling, bilingual text fit, reduced flashing and non-color cues for M6 product-gate surfaces.
acceptance: Keyboard and mouse basics work for product gate surfaces
UI scaling, long text, Chinese/English layout, reduced flashing and non-color danger/lamp cues are checked
Key audio cues have visual alternatives or documented blockers
React and Pixi remain read-model consumers and never repair authoritative simulation state

history.details: Instantiated from reviewed WM-0084 M6 DAG; keep proposed until WM-0085 is verified and done.

Implemented M6 input/a11y baseline evidence: visible non-color alert cues, real bilingual title, Web/Desktop e2e for pointer/keyboard, scaling, text fit, reduced flashing and no audio-only cues; required checks pass.

changes_requested: High: Desktop Electron product surface lacks automated pointer selection and keyboard pan/zoom evidence required by WM-0092 Web/Windows input baseline. Web e2e covers canvas pointer plus Equal/ArrowLeft, but desktop-shell e2e only checks launch, bilingual text, non-color cues, media/animation absence and overflow. Add Electron dev/packaged surface input assertions before verification. Other required checks passed.

Addressed reviewer request by adding Desktop Electron pointer selection and keyboard pan/zoom evidence in dev and packaged shells; updated report/design notes; pnpm typecheck, desktop e2e, full e2e, taskctl validate, git diff --check and pnpm quality pass.

verified: No blocking findings. Verified prior desktop input gap is fixed: desktop-shell e2e calls the repaired baseline from both dev and packaged Electron launches and now dispatches canvas pointer selection plus Equal/ArrowLeft keyboard checks while only reading debug/UI state. Web/Desktop a11y coverage, bilingual title, non-color cues, no audio/video cues, reduced flashing/overflow checks, and read-model boundaries are intact. Required checks pass.
Reviewer verified WM-0092 after changes-requested fix; integration gates passed: validate-handoff, taskctl validate/status, git diff --check and pnpm quality. Branch is ready for main merge.

objective: Establish input and accessibility baseline evidence for Web and Windows product-gate surfaces.
risksAndFileOwnership: Risk: accessibility scope expands into full tutorial or full localization overhaul.
Risk: color-only lamp/danger states pass visually but fail accessibility baseline.
File ownership is limited to UI, renderer input and accessibility docs.
ownerGate: Block on player-critical state conveyed by color or sound alone
Block if UI mutates authoritative simulation state
Block if M7 tutorial or public playtest work is needed
m0M5RegressionCoverage: No simulation authority or hash baseline changes
benchmarkImpact: No benchmark baseline impact except possible UI smoke timing notes.
reviewRouting: client-engineer completes to reviewer; reviewer verifies input/a11y coverage and authority boundaries.
rollbackModel: Revert input/a11y changes and record missing baseline as product-gate blocker.

sparkEligibility.reason: Accessibility baseline requires product-surface judgment and Playwright evidence, not Spark.

review.summary: No blocking findings. Verified prior desktop input gap is fixed: desktop-shell e2e calls the repaired baseline from both dev and packaged Electron launches and now dispatches canvas pointer selection plus Equal/ArrowLeft keyboard checks while only reading debug/UI state. Web/Desktop a11y coverage, bilingual title, non-color cues, no audio/video cues, reduced flashing/overflow checks, and read-model boundaries are intact. Required checks pass.

integration.summary: Reviewer verified WM-0092 after changes-requested fix; integration gates passed: validate-handoff, taskctl validate/status, git diff --check and pnpm quality. Branch is ready for main merge.
